summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, Democracy / anti-fascism, AI / computing. The strongest items in this run are LA mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt reposts scathing AI-generated ad, Alleged suicide note written by Jeffrey Epstein unsealed by federal judge, and Justice department can keep 2020 ballots FBI seized from Fulton county, judge rules.

mainItems.title: LA mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt reposts scathing AI-generated ad
mainItems.summary: Ex-TV star launches viral video a day before debate with mayor Karen Bass and city council member Nithya Raman Los Angeles mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt reposted a scathing AI-generated ad on X on Tuesday, which cast the city as a bleak…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Alleged suicide note written by Jeffrey Epstein unsealed by federal judge
mainItems.summary: Epstein’s cellmate in New York City says he found note after convicted sexual offender attempted suicide in July 2019 A federal judge unsealed an alleged suicide note written by Jeffrey Epstein on Wednesday, the first time the document has…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Justice department can keep 2020 ballots FBI seized from Fulton county, judge rules
mainItems.summary: The heavily Democratic Fulton county has been at the center of Trump’s false claims of election fraud in 2020 The federal government can keep the 2020 election ballots from Georgia’s Fulton county seized by the FBI from a warehouse near…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Mira Murati tells the court that she couldn’t trust Sam Altman’s words
mainItems.summary: Mira Murati, OpenAI's former CTO, has testified under oath that CEO Sam Altman lied to her about the safety standards for a new AI model. In a video deposition shown during the ongoing Musk v.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Google's Gemma 4 AI models get 3x speed boost by predicting future tokens
mainItems.summary: Up to 3x the speed with no loss of quality—is it too good to be true?
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to local models, open tooling, and hands-on experimentation.

mainItems.title: Stranger Than Heaven's new combat system sounds pretty complicated, but looks absolutely amazing
mainItems.summary: At this evening's (or morning's, depending on how you look at things) Xbox Presents, we got a very in-depth look at Ryu Ga Gotoku's upcoming title, Stranger Than Heaven .
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Federal Complaint Accuses NEA of Bias, Allowing Threats Against Jewish Members
mainItems.summary: A federal civil rights complaint charges that the National Education Association, which represents 3 million educators across the country, created hostile work environments and discriminated against its Jewish members. The Louis D.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Report: SpaceX IPO gives Musk unchecked power and forbids investor lawsuits
mainItems.summary: Anyone who buys into SpaceX IPO must waive right to sue the firm, report says.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: Ohio Is Making Sure Industry-Recognized Credentials Pay Off. Your State Can, Too
mainItems.summary: Delivering high-quality career-technical education has quickly moved up the policy agenda across the country, with many states turning to industry credentials to help build students’ professional skill sets and signal to employers that…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Scientists Gave ‘Aggressive’ Fish Psychedelic Drugs. A Breakthrough Came Next
mainItems.summary: “We really had no idea what we were getting ourselves into,” said one researcher involved in the first-of-its-kind study that dosed fish with psilocybin, the component in magic mushrooms.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: Why Daredevil: Born Again Season 3 Will Adapt “The Devil in Cell-Block D”
mainItems.summary: Showrunner Dario Scardapane says Born Again has been building towards the story for quite some time The post Why Daredevil: Born Again Season 3 Will Adapt “The Devil in Cell-Block D” appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

mainItems.title: These companies help parents try to pick their babies' traits. Experts are wary
mainItems.summary: Prospective parents can now pick embryos based on risk predictions for thousands of diseases and odds for specific traits. But should they?
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: The simple, one-tool trick to make bright frosting : Putting a popular method to the test.
mainItems.summary: If you’ve ever dumped a whole bottle of food coloring into a batch of frosting only to be left with a pale pastel hue instead of the deep, vibrant colors you were going for, you know that making bright frosting can be a challenge.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Lower-stakes, but high fit for the maker/craft side of your brain.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

new.title: Constellation
new.why: A little more cerebral and mood-heavy, but it scratches the identity / reality-slippage itch.

new.title: The Devil’s Hour
new.why: Dark, twisty, and structurally ambitious without feeling like homework.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

older.title: Continuum
older.why: A cleaner old-school sci-fi fit if you want procedural momentum wrapped around time-travel politics.

older.title: Person of Interest
older.why: Starts procedural and then quietly becomes one of the smartest AI / surveillance shows on television.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: Solve a murder mystery by flying people around on your giant crane in the Disco Elysium and Chinese folktale inspired The Crane Rider's Tale
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing at James's request.

highlights.title: 12 unexpected sourdough bread recipes that aren't crusty loaves: From sourdough flatbread to sourdough milk bread — and even sourdough babka.
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing, the kind of bread-nerd rabbit hole worth keeping.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
